name: qits
//user: Run as the non-root `dev` user (defined in the Dockerfile). updateRemoteUserUID remaps dev's uid/gid to the host user on machines where it isn't 1000, so bind-mounted source and the shared Claude volume stay owned by the same uid the workspace containers run as.
//: App ingress so the host browser reaches the qits UI (Quinoa :4200 -> Quarkus :8080). Workspace containers publish nothing; qits reaches them by DNS name on qits-net.
//initialize: Create the external qits-net + shared volumes on the HOST before the stack comes up (idempotent). qits-net is shared with sibling workspace containers; qits_shared_dot_claude is the shared Claude Code login; m2/pnpm are shared build caches. Also capture the host's timezone into .devcontainer/.env (gitignored) — the compose file interpolates it into the container's TZ so devcontainer wall-clock matches the host (and qits passes its zone on to every workspace container).
initializeCommand: docker network inspect qits-net >/dev/null 2>&1 || docker network create qits-net; for v in qits_shared_dot_claude qits_shared_m2 qits_shared_pnpm; do docker volume inspect $v >/dev/null 2>&1 || docker volume create $v; done; tz=$(cat /etc/timezone 2>/dev/null || timedatectl show -p Timezone --value 2>/dev/null || true); echo "TZ=${tz:-Etc/UTC}" > .devcontainer/.env
//postCreate: Build once so the `domain` module is resolvable (fresh-checkout step from CLAUDE.md), then seed demo data. Seeding is pure host-side data setup (workspace containers are provisioned lazily on first use), so the seeds run directly — best-effort, so a seed failure never fails create. See .devcontainer/postcreate.sh.

vscode.//settings: Point the Java Language Server (redhat.java / JDT-LS's embedded m2e resolver) at .devcontainer/m2-settings.xml, whose <localRepository> is the shared /caches/m2 volume. Without this the LS ignores the compose MAVEN_OPTS and resolves into the default ~/.m2 on the ephemeral container layer, so every clean devcontainer re-downloads all dependencies from Central. The terminal ./mvnw already uses /caches/m2 via MAVEN_OPTS; this unifies the LS onto the same cache.
